sensitivity: caries: 83.9%; PARLs: 89.8%; bone level detection (bitewing): 95.5%; bone level detection (periapical): 87.3%
specificity: caries: 90.2%; PARLs: 97.3%; bone level detection (bitewing): 94.0%; bone level detection (periapical): 92.1%
intended_use: Smile Dx® is a computer-assisted detection (CADe) software designed to aid dentists in the review of digital files of bitewing and periapical radiographs of permanent teeth. It is intended to aid in t
intended_use_full: Smile Dx® is a computer-assisted detection (CADe) software designed to aid dentists in the review of digital files of bitewing and periapical radiographs of permanent teeth. It is intended to aid in the detection and segmentation of suspected dental findings which include: caries, periapical radiolucencies (PARL), restorations, and dental anatomy. Smile Dx® is also intended to aid dentists in the measurement (in millimeter and percentage measurements) of mesial and distal bone levels associated with each tooth.
target_condition: suspected caries, periapical radiolucencies (PARL), restorations, and abnormal bone levels in dental radiographs
ground_truth: consensus labels of four US licensed dentists
other_metrics: wAFROC (caries): 0.908 [control: 0.781, Δθ +0.127 (p<0.001)]; wAFROC (PARLs): 0.947 [control: 0.849, Δθ +0.098 (p<0.001)]; Dice coefficient (caries): 0.74 [0.72-0.76]; Dice coefficient (PARLs): 0.77 [0.74-0.80]; Dice coefficient (normal anatomy): 0.84 [0.83-0.85]; Dice coefficient (restorations): 0.87 [0.85-0.90]; bone loss measurement mean absolute error (bitewing): 0.30 mm [0.29-0.32 mm]; bone loss measurement mean absolute error (periapical): 2.6% [2.4%-2.8%]; sensitivity improvement (caries): +19.6% [12.8%-26.4%]; sensitivity improvement (PARLs): +19.1% [13.6%-24.7%]; specificity improvement (caries): +16.7% [13.5%-19.9%]; specificity improvement (PARLs): +4.7% [3%-6.4%]
